description: I stopped to look at a pewee on a wire alongside the road. By pewee, I mean smallish but not Empid-small flycatcher, with long wings and overall olive-greenish coloration. The belly was pale yellowish green fading to whitish chest and throat. Slight olive smudging on sides of chest. Mandiple entirely pinkish-orange. No markings noted on vent/undertail coverts. Maxilla black. Bill relatively long and narrow. Wingbars moderate intensity and white, with upper and lower fairly equal in intensity. No eyering. Back moderately green. The bird was initially in poor light, when it was heard to call peee-weeee, without any burriness. Later, it sang PEE-oo-weee twice, again clear, "thin" notes without any burriness. No tail wagging or wing flicking.
